metadata.title: 12. Morphology and Adaptations in Higher Plants

questions.question: Which among the following kinds of roots are found in Sugarcane?
questions.explanation: In Maize and Sugarcane, roots arise from the first few nodes of the stem and penetrate obliquely down in to the soil and give support to the plant. They are called Stilt roots.

questions.question: In context with biology, which among the following is a Zoophyte?

options.label: A
options.text: A plant that resembles an animal

options.label: B
options.text: An animal that resembles a plant

options.label: C
options.text: A plant that grows on an animal

options.label: D
options.text: An animal that grows on a plant

answer.label: B
answer.text: An animal that resembles a plant

questions.explanation: A zoophyte is an animal that visually resembles a plant.

questions.question: Which among the following is a millet?
questions.explanation: Millets are a group of highly variable small-seeded grasses, widely grown around the world as cereal crops or grains for fodder and human food. It is usually considered a separate cereal, but sometimes also known as great millet.

questions.question: The Stomata in the aquatic plants is ________?

options.label: B
options.text: Found & available on both the surfaces

options.label: C
options.text: Found & available on upper surface only

options.label: D
options.text: Found and available only on lower surface

answer.label: C
answer.text: Found & available on upper surface only

questions.explanation: The Stomata in the aquatic plants is found and available on upper surface only. Stomata are crucial for plant functioning as they play fundamental role in the regulation of gas exchange between the plant and its surrounding environment.

questions.question: Onion, daffodil, tulip are which kind of stems?
questions.explanation: Onion, daffodil, tulip are bulb stems. The best example of a true bulb is the onion. The rings which we see when we slice an onion in half are actually leaves modified to hold food for the bulb when it is dormant.
